events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`, keeping it scoped to the staged belt-tension defect and the manufacturer guide reference.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /tmp/answer.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"staged\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 0,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.89,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Installed belt tension is outside the manufacturer deflection specification.\",
    \"Proceeding without setting tension to the OEM guide risks belt slip, heat buildup, bearing overload, and premature belt or sheave failure.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not return the drive to service with the current belt tension condition.\",
    \"Use the manufacturer-specified belt tension or deflection method and calibrated tension gauge to adjust tension before operation.\",
    \"Inspect sheave alignment, belt condition, sheave wear, and coupling condition before final tensioning and test run.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed condition is already identified as belt tension off by deflection measurement. At the staged point, the correct approach is not replacement by default or operation as-is; it is to verify the measured deflection against the manufacturer guide, correct the belt tension with the specified gauge/method, and inspect related drive components that can cause or be damaged by improper tension.\",
  \"references\": [\"Manufacturer belt drive service guides\", \"OEM deflection/tension specification\"]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.tmp /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
→ /app
